Thank you very much. Thank you very much. Thank you very much. Oh, wow. You make me feel like a rock star. Today as we are learning a sales presentation that is very powerful, you may have questions and as you have questions, I would like for you to ask your question in the microphone and I am going to ask Ms. Rebecca Joy if she would take care of the microphone and make sure that it is in your face and please try to remember, don't ask the question unless you have the microphone. Are you ready to learn some extremely powerful words and phrases and methods of convincing people to do what you want them to do? I am trying to choose my words carefully. Before I get started, I must tell you that when you know the things that I am going to share with you, you can literally abuse it. I certainly request that you do not because the power to influence others is an awesome power. If you use it for good, you are going to be a wonderful human being and you will change a lot of lives. But you can also use it just flat out to sell and I have seen people that can sell things they do not believe in and you know you hear this stuff that says well if you really believe in your product you can sell it. That is true. I can sell it if I do not believe in it. You know why? Because I have learned a system. I have learned a method and I have learned a way to present words in such a fashion that it moves an audience. My expertise is selling from the platform. I am going to share a lot of these inside secrets with you today. We are doing a filming of this because people who come to my three day training, I promise them that I would videotape all the chunks that I teach. I have a very expensive training, in fact it is as far as I know it is the most expensive training in this country. I charge $25,000 for speaker training. I have had people say well now that is a lot of money. Well good gracious. If I teach you to make $250,000 are you willing to pay 10% of what you make to learn how to be in a seminar business, be a public speaker, be a million dollar speaker? I said I had somebody come up to me one time and said well if you are so confident that this is going to work and by the way I am, then why don't you just let me pay you out of the profits that I make as a speaker. Now you need to write that down because if you are a public speaker that will come up over and over and over again. But I am so confident that I know two things. I know that if you come for free you won't learn one thing. You will just absorb because you have no commitment. But I tell you what I will do, if you pay 20% to me now I will carry 80% of it. I am that confident that this is going to work for you. Which means you pay $5,000 and you come to a training. Right now our training is three days long. We are going to take it to four days long and then we are going to take it to five days long. And you don't pay me one more dollar if this does not work for you. And the other thing is at our training you get a 100% guarantee on two levels. Number one the second thing is if this does not work for you you will mean nothing additional. All you would have paid was $5,000. But the first $5,000 has an unconditional guarantee that if you don't like it after you tend the entire thing I will give you your money back. That's how confident I am. And you know why I am that confident? Because I have never taught anything in my life that works. Any more effective than what I am going to share with you. Now in our training we have four areas that we teach. Number one we teach the seminar business and this is just how this whole industry works that you can make money in. Number two we teach product development because the key is you need product. Number three we teach marketing concepts. How you market the products that you develop. And number four we teach developing your powerful killer sales presentation. Now believe it or not that's all we are going to talk about today is developing a sales presentation that you can get your audience to do what you want to do. So are you ready to go? Yes. All right then I want you to get a clean piece of paper which means if you have a pad of paper I will tell you when I want you to have a clean one. I want you to flip over to a new page and I want you to draw a line all the way across the top with two end markers there. It's just a straight line. It has a beginning and it has an end. This represents the timeline of your sales presentation. The timeline of your sales presentation. Obviously it has a beginning and it has an end. I am not going to put a number of minutes on it because sometimes you will be giving a three day presentation and you divide out the timeline based on what you are going to do and the time that you have allocated. You may be talking to a Chamber of Commerce and you may have 15 minutes. Then it's a timeline. What are you going to say in your 15 minutes? So what we are going to do is constantly going back to that timeline and if you notice the small things that I do some of you will notice and some of you will not. But I will try to point them out. I use body language and if I am walking over here a lot of times this means that we are at the beginning of your sales presentation. When I walk all the way to the other end it means that we are talking about the end of your sales presentation. I will walk hopefully to the point in time that we will be talking about where you are in your sales presentation. I don't always do it but I will try to point out that we are. You began all presentations with the end and this is contrary to what you have probably always been told all your life. You began with the end in mind meaning what is it that you are going to be selling in your sales presentation? Any ideas about what you are going to be selling? Yes? Rebecca? You are almost fired already. I would be selling the concept of being a millionaire and being willing to give back to create more of who I am. Okay. You are going to be being a millionaire. Now is there a process that a person has to go through to be a millionaire? Yes. Obviously the answer is yes. I will shake my head this way when the answer is yes. So yes there is a process that you have to go through to be a millionaire. So if you are going to teach people how to be a millionaire you must teach them that process. Would you agree with me on that? Yes. Alright now if you are going to teach people to share you are going to have to teach some emotional mindset strategies to convince people to quit being the creep that they are and be a wonderful human being. Because there are a lot of people out here that you will speak to that are actually less than what the qualities are of the people we have in this room right now. So the object is wherever people are you want to take them to a higher level. Alright now let's be a little bit more specific. That is philosophically where you want to take them. What is it that you want to sell that you are going to charge a fee for? Anybody have any input for that? Knowledge. Knowledge. You just weren't quick enough there Rebecca. Okay. The knowledge with the actual experience that has been experienced. The processing. Okay. I appreciate that. Y'all are all getting warm and fuzzy on me. I want to know what you are going to hand me when I give you a check. I don't know that you are going to hand this but it is the system. What people are buying is the system. Can I touch it? Can I feel it? When I write you a check what do I get? A product. Is that right? Information. Okay. We are heading in totally the wrong direction here. I am going to teach you how to sell a product and people will write you a check or they will put it on their credit card. What is it that you are selling? That is what I want to know. Are you selling some books and tapes? Are you selling a live training? Are you selling I will be your mentor? Are you selling that Robert Allen might be a mentor? What are you selling? Because that is why I sell like crazy. I know what I am selling at the end of my presentation and that is where I start. Okay so let's start on your product. Somewhere on your notes I want you to put product. Now what are I mean I have almost given you all the answers but I am going to see how much you were listening. Seminars, in other words live trainings could be something. I will be your mentor. Is that something you might want to sell? Okay. There are many products here that you will add. Now if you are selling someone else's product maybe they have all this developed in which that is already a program that is in place and you could sell someone else's program and that is the end point. You start with the end in mind. What is it you are trying to sell? Now here is another product. What if you are not trying to get them to write a check? What if you are merely the entire purpose of your word presentation is to get them to make a phone call? In other words down here when you get ready to do your quote close all you want them to do is give me your email address and I will ask someone to call you to see if you are qualified. What if you merely want them to call an 800 number then that becomes your product. Well if that is your product that has everything, it changes everything all the way up the line about what you would say. You start with the end in mind. Now what I am going to do today now that we have kind of thrown out a few things I am going to walk through what we call chunks. A chunk is something that you can memorize. It would be verbatim or it is just I know exactly the intention of this chunk so you can say your own words but you can have the stretched out version or you can have the condensed version. Now if you are going to speak for four hours you are going to have the stretched out version. And if they give you an hour you are going to have to shrink it down in a condensed version but you still want the certain points that you are going to plug in that timeline. Now while we are talking about that timeline I am going to show you the cardinal sin that most speakers do. They have all this stuff that they want to say up front and then they get down here and they get right and they have not done their clothes yet and all of a sudden here they are and they have no time left and guess what gets cut out. You cut out your clothes so what you want to do in your timeline you need to practice closing whatever it is that you are going to sell and find out exactly how much time that takes and you need to know right to the second how much time that takes. So you know a promoter will tell you when you need to end or whoever is in charge of the meeting will tell you you need to end at this time. Okay and you time it and if your clothes takes you five minutes then you back up at five minutes and you put a time right there and you know. I don't care where you are in your presentation when you get to that time you interrupt whatever you are and you start in your clothes. A professional speaker there is absolutely no reason, none that you should ever go over, none. In my opinion and I have seen people who claim to be professionals but go over and go way over is because they don't understand the timeline themselves. They go off on tangents and they don't have this end in mind. You need a particular point in your timeline that you know you have to start your clothes. What I am going to share with you now is information that I don't care where you speak you will be ranked in the top four or five speakers if you follow this format. I don't care what your topic is I don't care who you compete with when you look at the evaluation you will be right on top and it is a secret. Now you have heard before when talking about a presentation or a speech, the first course I ever took in college was speech and I hate to tell you this but I don't hate to tell you this I made an A. What I hate to tell you is I had never ever made an A. In my entire schooling I had never made an A. I quit high school at age seventeen. I figured you know I was born to walk the line I didn't need any more schooling so I quit I joined the Air Force and when I got out I was labeled a quitter. Now that's not a label that you really want to have. So I thought the only way I can quit being a quitter is finish something. I decided to go back to college and I graduated from college. I graduated from college three years from the day I started. I worked full time. I went four nights a week for four hours. I went on Saturday. I went on my lunch hour and the truth be known on Tuesdays and Thursdays I took off of work and I went over there and went to class. But I never made an A in my life. My father had a rule he said if you don't make all C's you don't get to watch television. I never watched television the entire time I was in high school. That's the reason I wanted to quit and get out of there. Now I made this A in speech and one of the things that they taught you, tell them what you're going to tell them. Tell them what you told them. Just a rule. Well I'm going to tell you how to turn that rule into the most powerful part of your presentation you can imagine. Let's begin with the first three things. What is it? Tell them what you're going to tell them. Tell them what you told them. Let's look at the first one. What does it mean to tell them what you're going to tell them? It simply means give them an overview of what you're going to tell them. It simply means you're going to be covering. Now we're going to combine that principle with something else that has to do with quality. I don't care what product you sell and what business you are in, you really need to follow this philosophy of business. Under promise and over deliver. Now what in the world does that mean? And I had somebody in my class say well whatever you're delivering when you promise it, don't promise that much. No that's not what it means at all. It means promise them everything that you think you can provide. Promise them what they want. Find out what your customer wants and tell them I'm going to give you that. But let that be an under promise compared to what they get. So tell them all the things that you're going to cover, but as you are delivering that, cover more. Now here's an example. If you're going to cover four ways, well let's just say if you're going to cover three ways to make money in your presentation. In the beginning is that today we're going to cover three ways, three separate sources of income. You can call it multiple sources of income if you'd like. And we'll cover three of these ways in such a manner that you no longer for the rest of your life will have to depend on one source of income. How many of you would like to have at least two sources of income? Could I show a hands on that? But what if I showed you how to get three sources of income? Now all of a sudden all your eggs aren't in one basket and would that make it a little bit more mentally secure when it comes to supporting your family? If one of them stopped, that's okay. I've got two more sources of income. So we'll cover three sources of income, not two, three. Time out. What have you covered four? Now you see what I'm talking about? You build up and tell them what you're going to tell them. I added, hey, I'm going to tell you how to get at least two, but I may go three. Now when I start telling them, I'm going to give them four. Is that called under promise, over deliver? You've got to make the different comparison here. And what I'll find over and over and over again is people went right from speech class to making a speech. They never studied the philosophy of business of under promise and over deliver. Consequently, you'll see about 98% of the speakers out here in the tell them what you're going to tell them. We're going to cover three ways of making money. And in the tell them section, they taught them three ways. And in the tell them what you told them, this is nothing but a review. And they were reminded, you know, I promise you guys teach you three ways and they would do a good job of learning those three ways. Think we did. Now if you'd like to buy my product, let's go. Okay, now they just delivered what was promised. I'm telling you, you always give more than you promised, but not at the expense of taking it out of your promise. You need to give them everything that they want in the promise, but then in the delivery, you over deliver. Then they when you get to tell them what you told them, you're able to remind them, you know, I don't, seriously, I don't get to speak to this high of a level audience very often. One I do, I find that I get to cover a lot more information because you're so smart. You're so receptive. You think faster than most. Consequently, I was able to cover this and this and this and these other things. It's called tell them what you're going to tell them, then over deliver what you promised. And then you get the opportunity to remind them of because of their receptiveness, you were able to give them way more than was promised. Now I call this give them a ruler to measure you by. Now under give them a ruler to measure you by, wonder what part of the three things that is. Tell them what you're going to tell them, tell them, tell them what you told them. Where's the ruler? Right up here. In the beginning, you need to tell people what they are going to learn today. You have just given them a ruler to measure you by. And I'll bet you that 60% of the speakers in their presentation, they do not tell people what they're going to learn today. But when you tell them what they're going to learn, that's your ruler. And they have something to measure you by. If you do not give them something to measure you by, they will measure you with all kinds of different rulers. And they say, boy, when John Childress came on, they gave him a rousin welcome, but I was at Zig Ziglar seminar, he doesn't even compare to the folks. That's not the ruler. The ruler is we are going to learn today an extremely powerful sales presentation. That's the ruler. And you won't compare me with anyone. You will compare me to did you learn a powerful sales presentation? And you will, because I'm going to lay it on you folks. And then I'm going to remind you down here of how much more we had, we learned than what was promised. It's called under promise, over deliver. Tell them what you're going to tell them. Tell them, tell them what you told them. All right. Good morning. I know that this is a different audience there. Let's try that again. Good morning. All right. And the thing I heard one time at a wealth seminar was that there's three levels. There's financial independence. That's where you don't need a job and you've got a steady stream of income that supports you in a comfortable lifestyle. So you're basically, you're not having to work a nine to five job. Then comes financial freedom. Financial freedom means that you've got a lifestyle that supports you, that you can go on six vacations a year, you can do what you want and basically have a good time. If you were teaching people how to become financial independent, what would their life be like? If you were teaching them communication skills, what would their life be like? If you were teaching them sales skills, what would their life be? Whatever you're teaching, what would their life be like if they had it? That's the way you start your presentation. Because I love people and I love talking about what I do and I will talk to you until I outnumber you. Alright, now, so do you understand the first chunk? It's called the American Dream. I don't care what you sell or what your presentation is, in the beginning you need a chunk that shows the light at the end of the tunnel. It shows where they would be, what their lifestyle would be. Okay, now, you're ready to start another chunk? I want you to notice a ton of voice. You can tell when I'm talking to you and when I'm giving my presentation. Okay, the American Dream of Financial Independence is alive and it is doing well. And whatever that dream might mean for you, you can have it if you simply take the time to learn how to get it. Now the first step in that process though is that we need to stop working so hard. I mean is that good news? Now I know that I'm already telling you things that are contrary to what you've been told all your life. What were we all told about working hard? Oh yeah, you work hard. Get a good education. You work hard and everything will be fine. Well what happened? We did get a good education. We work hard. Let me show you something. I want everybody to participate. How many of you are hard workers? How many of you are wealthy? Two or three out of the whole bunch. Evidently, working hard doesn't work. I rest my case. But guess what? That may be contrary to what you've been told all your life. It is. What we've got to learn to do today is to work smart. Now I know you've heard that before. In today's context, working smart means getting involved in something that will give us income. Might be getting involved in something that would give us some tax breaks. It means getting involved in something that will build a savings for us. It means getting involved in something that we can own things that go up in value. And it means getting involved in something that we don't have to have so much of our money involved in. Now how many of you like to get involved in a program like that? All right. Time out. That's a chunk. It's called working hard doesn't work. Now what do you think? What is the power of that chunk? Any ideas on that? Okay, I'm not going to take all of your comments, just one. It gets them in an agreement mode, something they automatically agree with. Nobody's going to say I don't work hard. No one's going to say they don't work hard. And by the way, if you are wealthy, do you want to put your hand in the air? No, you do not. Consequently, you have made a comparison here. The whole world sees working hard doesn't work. How many of you are wealthy? Maybe I have my hand in the air normally. But if there are other hands in the air, only one or two of you agree with, never say, never say, no, that's not what I wanted to happen here because you will have, okay? But the comparison was obvious. I rest my case. Working hard doesn't work. Now there's something else I said. I know that I'm already saying things that are contrary to what you have been told all of your life. Am I setting myself up of letting you know that I'm not in agreement with what we were told? Okay, now if you do not say that, people will argue with you in your presentation. Now I've had professional speakers come up to me and say, John, what do you say when people ask this? I said, well, I don't know. I've never had anybody ask that. Well sure you have. You know, you've been speaking out here 20 years. Surely they've asked you that sometime. Well no, in 20 years no one ever asked me that. And it frustrates them because they have been asked that so many times that they just said, well surely you've been asked it once. I've never been asked that. Why? Because I eliminate any confrontation in the beginning by letting them know. I'm telling you things that are contrary. So instead of them saying, now that's not what I've heard, they already know I'm telling them things that they have not heard before. I'm setting myself up, letting them know I'm going to teach them some different things today. And I'm also letting them know by a visual that what they have been taught, what you have been taught is probably wrong when it comes to making money. Because if you had been taught the wealth formula and you implemented it, you would be wealthy. And we'll learn that in a moment as I teach that. Okay, that's called working hard doesn't work. How did that end? I'm working hard. Oh, how many of you would like to get involved in a program like that? Now I did something very unique here. When I said working smart, now I know that you've heard that term before, haven't you? Working smart. So I define what it means today. That way I don't have anybody ready to say excuse me, but that's not what working smart means really. Working smart means I don't have those arguments. Why? Because I chose my words carefully. I said, because in today's con, I know you've heard that before. So in today's context, working smart means, and I defined it what it means today and you won't have anybody argue with you. Now guess what I'm going to define it as based on what I'm going to teach in a few moments, based on what I want you to learn. And this is the second round of I'm going to tell you what I'm going to tell you, and then I'm going to deliver and you're going to be happy about it. And I said working smart today means getting involved in something that allows us to have extra income, tax savings, bill the savings, own things that go up in value, and use somebody else's money to do it with. How many of you like to get involved in a program like that and I shot my hand in the air quickly and so did you? If you want people to raise your hand, you shoot your hand in the air, but will people normally not want to raise their hand? There are occasions you don't want everybody in the room to raise their hand. So in that case, raise your hand about halfway and do it fairly slowly and you'll get a few hands in the audience come up about halfway. But when you want all the hands in there, you need a stealth. Have them about like this and then you shoot them up and then everybody raises your hand. You learn to control an audience. Am I going too fast? Is this good stuff? All right. Now how many of you would like to sign up for a program like that? That is called a trial close. They just made a decision, didn't they? Now I did not say how many of you would like to go back to our enrollment section, fill out an application and buy our product, did I? Or did I? How many of you would like to get involved in a program like that? It is a trial close and it is a mental commitment on your audience's part. Well, if working smart is going to be involved in that, why hasn't it worked for us? I think one of the reasons that most people haven't reached financial independence is because of taxes. They just keep raising our taxes, don't they? And inflation. Inflation. Now what is inflation right around? What is it? I started 2 or 3 percent. I heard 6 percent. I heard 4 or 5 percent and I even heard a 10 percent. I can get so many different answers. This is almost like trying to ask the government, isn't it? You know what the government needs to do? They need to go to the grocery store two weeks in a row. Are the prices going up? I mean what were you paying for a loaf of bread 10 years ago? 49, 59 cents. How much are you paying for a loaf of bread today? $3.60? $2.00? $3.00? Something like that. What is it, this much larger? Oh, it's the same size, isn't it? Oh, and in some cases it's even smaller, right? It's the same product. It's not any better. It's not any worse. It just costs a lot more money. That's inflation, isn't it? Now inflation doesn't mean things become more valuable. It just means it costs more money to buy the same thing. So we get a pay raise. We think we're getting ahead. No, I have to make more money to buy the very same thing. That's the effects of inflation. And you know, how about, by the way, you make more money to buy the same thing, but if you ever notice the tax system is based on the more money you make, the higher the tax is, right? So who do you think is in charge of this inflation? The people collecting the higher tax rates, right? Duh! Now, have you heard all this political jargon about the new tax program? They talk about reducing income tax, or they talk about changing taxes. But the reality is the government needs more money. Now, if they get to get more money, the taxes have to go up. Whatever the, you know, they're always talking about these proposed tax changes. Now under the proposed tax changes, how many of you think that the taxes are going to go up? I do. Okay. Now, let me ask you, let me tell you something that you never thought about. There only got three classes of people in this country. You got the poor people, you got the people in the middle, and then you got the wealthy people. Now how many of you believe that whatever these proposed tax changes are, how many of you think they're going to raise the taxes on the poor people? I see a few hands here, I disagree. What's the benefit? How do you help the country by taxing people who don't have any money? Well, how many of you think they're going to raise the taxes on the wealthy people? Okay. Well, there's only three groups of people. Where do you think the tax increases are going to be focused? Right smack in the middle, working class, blue collar, the average middle class of America, right? Now by the way, how many of you are paying federal income taxes right now? Can I see your show of hands? How many of you like to stop? Wait a minute. I got more hands for that question and I got how many of you are paying out here. Okay. Now if you don't want to pay any more than federal income taxes, it looks to me like you got one or two choices. Either become rich or poor. Now which would you prefer? I chose wealthy. Okay, time out. What is the chunk that you can either insert, play with, have fun with, or you can just condense it down to, you know, there's two things keeping us from being where we want to be. They keep raising the taxes and then with this inflation and you could go on or you can spread it out and you can have fun with the reason that people aren't wealthy. Now what I want you to see is this, with that chunk, the first chunk says if you would take the time to learn, you could be wealthy. And now we've addressed, why aren't you wealthy? Well, between taxes continue to rise and inflation, you know, I've seen this example going up the down elevator escalator. You know, you're just trying to go up this escalator, it's going down. Is that what inflation is? You know, the value of the dollar keeps going down. You have to work harder and harder and harder just to stay at the same place. But if you want to get ahead, now the taxes hit you and you're just going faster and faster and faster and you're just not getting anywhere. What we've got to do is learn a system that allows us to get us out of that system. How many of you would like to know a principle that would make inflation work for you instead of against you? Would that be value? Now think about what I just said. We've all felt inflation in our pocketbook. We felt it. What would happen if inflation instead of working against you worked for you? There is a process that you can do. Wealthy people have learned it. When you learn it, you hope that the inflation rate goes higher. And when the people who are wealthy want the inflation rate to go up, guess what happens to the inflation rate? It normally goes up. Let me share that piece of information with you. I learned this when I started speaking internationally. In a lot of countries, not in the free world, but in a lot of countries, things are not adjusted for inflation. By the way, there is one word in the free country. There's one thing in this country that is not adjusted for inflation. And if you know the correct answer, you can become literally filthy rich. What is it that is not adjusted for inflation? Mortgage. Mortgage. That's half correct. Real estate is not it. The word is debt. It's your half right. The word is debt. Now let me show you what I'm talking about. Here's a very simple plan. Why don't you go out and buy a million dollars worth of real estate? You don't have any money? Then borrow it. You got a million dollars worth of real estate, you owe one million dollars on it. How much equity do you have? Zero. Now inflation comes along. It costs more money to buy the same thing. Now the price of the real estate goes up. What happens to the debt? It stays the same. What's this in between my two hands? Equity. Powerful. Powerful. Wealthy people have learned to go into debt. That seems strange doesn't it? You think most wealthy people owe nobody and owe no money? No. Wealthy people owe everybody. Why? Because debt is not adjusted to inflation. But the asset you bought with the debt is adjusted, which means the prices continue to rise of the asset but the debt remains the same. Now that principle could make you filthy rich, just master it. Time out. What did we learn in that chunk? Are you seeing by the way that some of these chunks you may want to use, some of these chunks you could leave out, but when you want an entire presentation these things are powerful. Because what will happen is people for the first time in their lives start seeing things they've never seen before. They've never seen them before whatsoever.
